category_title: Quietly observant students who listen to stories or watch peers solve math problems
minimal_context: The student is sitting at their desk with an adapted book for an independent silent reading activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our independent silent reading activity. Your adapted book is on your desk.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly and watches other students complete their tasks.)

TUTOR: Please begin reading.

STUDENT: (Looks around the classroom, leaving the book untouched.)

TUTOR: You should be reading your book now.

STUDENT: (The adapted book remains closed on the desk while the student stares straight ahead.)

options.A: Provide a two-pocket folder where the left side is dedicated to reading tasks to be completed and the right to tasks finished each day.
options.B: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the independent reading activity throughout the daily classroom sessions.
options.C: Provide speech-generating devices to enable the student to 'read aloud' when called on during the independent reading activity.
options.D: Use a restorative approach during conflict resolution meetings: make the student aware of peers' feelings, encourage acknowledgment of impact, and provide reparation opportunities.
